mission: Define and implement a product-neutral schema-first file processing capability with driver-backed extraction and repo CLI proof.

phaseStatusMeaning.completed: The phase is documented and its packet-level exit criteria are satisfied.
phaseStatusMeaning.pending: The phase is ready for an implementation agent to execute.
phaseStatusMeaning.blocked: The phase cannot proceed without a named decision, dependency, or fixture.

keyDecisions.decision: @beep/file-processing lives in packages/foundation/capability/file-processing only after the foundation/capability gate is proven.
keyDecisions.rationale: The package owns product-neutral operation contracts and IR, not external engines, product semantics, or repo operations.

keyDecisions.decision: P1 is a minimum vertical proof across capability, drivers, and repo CLI, not a contract-only package.
keyDecisions.rationale: Foundation capability promotion requires at least two named current consumers importing the package.

keyDecisions.decision: Tika, libpff, Poppler, Gotenberg/LibreOffice, Docling, and similar engines are driver boundaries.
keyDecisions.rationale: External engines and process/HTTP sidecars must not leak into capability schemas or product slices.

keyDecisions.decision: OCR is represented as a strategy flag and skip reason in V1, but no OCR driver is implemented in V1.
keyDecisions.rationale: The first packet focuses on deterministic file extraction and archive export before layout/OCR workflows.

keyDecisions.decision: beep files process writes run.json, sources.jsonl, failures.jsonl, coverage.json, text references, and child artifact manifests encoded from @beep/file-processing schemas.
keyDecisions.rationale: The CLI proof needs deterministic, auditable output for fixtures and corpus coverage calibration.

openQuestions.question: Will the first libpff proof use a generated PST fixture, a public sample fixture, or an engine-unavailable availability proof?
openQuestions.default: Use a generated fixture when feasible; otherwise record a typed fixture-unavailable deferral and still prove error translation.

openQuestions.question: Does @beep/file-processing need an explicit /node entrypoint in V1?
openQuestions.default: Do not add /node in P1 unless implementation proves CLI-owned filesystem adapters are insufficient.

openQuestions.question: Which fixture generator creates DOC, DOCX, RTF, HTML, PDF, text, Markdown, and image metadata fixtures?
openQuestions.default: Prefer generated synthetic fixtures committed or generated through repo test helpers, with no private corpus content.

knownGaps: No engine availability has been tested yet.
HTML was promoted into V1 during packet hardening because the local corpus contains substantial exported email HTML.
The local corpus has XLS/XLSX and DOCM inputs that are known non-core inputs for V1.
PDF text-layer status for the local corpus has not been verified.

drivers.v1Role: Broad text and metadata extraction through Tika Server
PST export to EML and JSONL metadata through libpff tooling

tooling.v1Role: Repo-operated manifest proof and corpus coverage surface

engineMatrix.laterRole: PDF diagnostics, bbox/text comparison, image extraction

phases.name: Packet Bootstrap
phases.exitCriteriaSummary: Goal packet created with package topology, operation model, driver split, CLI proof, and V1 cutline.

phases.name: Packet Hardening
phases.exitCriteriaSummary: Goal packet hardened with architecture precedence, vertical proof sequencing, engine matrix, manifest contract, and error boundaries.

phases.name: Minimum Vertical File-Processing Proof
phases.exitCriteriaSummary: @beep/file-processing is scaffolded and imported by at least two real consumers, with the smallest driver and CLI manifest proof.

phases.name: Tika Driver Completion
phases.exitCriteriaSummary: @beep/tika implements broad extraction and metadata capabilities for all non-PST V1 formats, including HTML.

phases.name: libpff Driver Completion
phases.exitCriteriaSummary: @beep/libpff exports PST files into child EML artifacts and JSONL metadata, or records an explicit typed fixture/engine deferral.

phases.name: Repo CLI Proof Completion
phases.exitCriteriaSummary: beep files process writes a schema-encoded manifest tree for generated fixtures and optional coverage profiling.

phases.name: Quality And Handoff
phases.exitCriteriaSummary: Affected quality gates pass and implementation evidence is recorded.

nextAction: Implement P1 by landing the minimum vertical file-processing proof across @beep/file-processing, at least two real consumers, and beep files process manifest output.
